question: How do you say 'I would like one coffee, please' in Japanese?
correctAnswer: {{ja-JP|コーヒーをひとつください。|Kōhī o hitotsu kudasai|KO-hi O HI-to-tsoo KU-da-sai}}
incorrectAnswers: {{ja-JP|コーヒーがほしい。|Kōhī ga hoshii|KO-hi GA HO-sheei}}
{{ja-JP|コーヒーをのむ。|Kōhī o nomu|KO-hi O NO-mu}}
{{ja-JP|コーヒーください、はやく。|Kōhī kudasai, hayaku|KO-hi KU-da-sai, HAYA-ku}}
explanation: '[Item] o hitotsu kudasai' — 'Please give me one [item]' — is the standard café order pattern.

question: How do you ask 'Where is the bathroom?' in Japanese?
correctAnswer: {{ja-JP|トイレはどこですか。|Toire wa doko desu ka|TOI-re WA DO-ko DE-su KA}}
incorrectAnswers: {{ja-JP|トイレがありますか。|Toire ga arimasu ka|TOI-re GA A-ri-ma-su KA}}
{{ja-JP|トイレはいつですか。|Toire wa itsu desu ka|TOI-re WA I-tsoo DE-su KA}}
{{ja-JP|トイレに行く。|Toire ni iku|TOI-re NI I-ku}}
explanation: 'Doko desu ka' means 'where is'. Replace 'toire' with any destination.

question: How do you ask 'How much is this?' in Japanese?
correctAnswer: {{ja-JP|これはいくらですか。|Kore wa ikura desu ka|KO-re WA I-ku-ra DE-su KA}}
incorrectAnswers: {{ja-JP|これは何ですか。|Kore wa nan desu ka|KO-re WA NAN DE-su KA}}
{{ja-JP|これはどこですか。|Kore wa doko desu ka|KO-re WA DO-ko DE-su KA}}
{{ja-JP|これはいつですか。|Kore wa itsu desu ka|KO-re WA I-tsoo DE-su KA}}
explanation: 'Ikura desu ka' — 'How much is it?' — is essential for shopping and markets.

question: How do you ask for the bill at a Japanese restaurant?
correctAnswer: {{ja-JP|お会計をお願いします。|Okaikei o onegaishimasu|O-kai-kay O O-ne-gai-shee-ma-su}}
incorrectAnswers: {{ja-JP|もっと食べます。|Motto tabemasu|MOT-to TA-be-ma-su}}
{{ja-JP|メニューをください。|Menyū o kudasai|ME-nyu O KU-da-sai}}
{{ja-JP|おいしいです。|Oishii desu|OI-sheei DE-su}}
explanation: 'Okaikei o onegaishimasu' — 'The bill, please' — is the polite restaurant phrase.

question: How do you say 'I don't understand' in Japanese?
correctAnswer: {{ja-JP|わかりません。|Wakarimasen|WA-ka-ri-ma-sen}}
incorrectAnswers: {{ja-JP|しりません。|Shirimasen|SHI-ri-ma-sen}}
{{ja-JP|ありません。|Arimasen|A-ri-ma-sen}}
{{ja-JP|できません。|Dekimasen|DE-ki-ma-sen}}
explanation: 'Wakarimasen' is polite 'I don't understand'. Use it freely — locals will often switch to simpler Japanese.

question: How do you say 'Do you speak English?' in Japanese?
correctAnswer: {{ja-JP|英語を話せますか。|Eigo o hanasemasu ka|EI-go O HA-na-se-ma-su KA}}
incorrectAnswers: {{ja-JP|英語がわかりますか。|Eigo ga wakarimasu ka|EI-go GA WA-ka-ri-ma-su KA}}
{{ja-JP|英語を勉強しますか。|Eigo o benkyō shimasu ka|EI-go O BEN-kyo SHEE-ma-su KA}}
{{ja-JP|英語は難しいですか。|Eigo wa muzukashii desu ka|EI-go WA MU-zu-ka-sheei DE-su KA}}
explanation: 'Eigo o hanasemasu ka?' — 'Can you speak English?' — a key phrase when you need help.

question: How do you say 'Turn left' in Japanese?
correctAnswer: {{ja-JP|左に曲がってください。|Hidari ni magatte kudasai|HI-da-ri NI MA-gat-te KU-da-sai}}
incorrectAnswers: {{ja-JP|右に曲がってください。|Migi ni magatte kudasai|MI-gi NI MA-gat-te KU-da-sai}}
{{ja-JP|まっすぐ行ってください。|Massugu itte kudasai|MAS-su-gu IT-te KU-da-sai}}
{{ja-JP|止まってください。|Tomatte kudasai|TO-mat-te KU-da-sai}}
explanation: 'Hidari' = left, 'migi' = right, 'massugu' = straight ahead — the three direction words.

question: In beginner Japanese, how do you say 'Good evening.'?
correctAnswer: {{ja-JP|こんばんは。|Konbanwa.|KON-ban-wa.}}
incorrectAnswers: {{ja-JP|お元気ですか。|Ogenki desu ka.|O-gen-ki DE-su KA.}}
{{ja-JP|元気です、ありがとうございます。|Genki desu, arigatou gozaimasu.|GEN-ki DE-su, A-ri-ga-toh GO-zai-ma-su.}}
{{ja-JP|とても元気です、ありがとうございます。|Totemo genki desu, arigatou gozaimasu.|TO-te-mo GEN-ki DE-su, A-ri-ga-toh GO-zai-ma-su.}}
explanation: This is a common greeting or farewell used as a fixed chunk in everyday conversation. Memorizing it whole makes it easier to sound natural.

question: In beginner Japanese, how do you say 'I have a reservation.'?
correctAnswer: {{ja-JP|予約があります。|Yoyaku ga arimasu.|YOYA-ku GA A-ri-ma-su.}}
incorrectAnswers: {{ja-JP|シングルルームはありますか。|Shinguru ruumu wa arimasu ka.|SHIN-gu-ru ROO-mu WA A-ri-ma-su KA.}}
{{ja-JP|ダブルルームはありますか。|Daburu ruumu wa arimasu ka.|DA-bu-ru ROO-mu WA A-ri-ma-su KA.}}
{{ja-JP|Wi-Fiのパスワードをお願いします。|Wi-Fi no pasuwaado wo onegaishimasu.|WI-FI NO PA-su-waa-do WO O-ne-gai-shee-ma-su.}}
explanation: This is standard accommodation language for front-desk conversations. It is polite and easy to reuse.

{{ja-JP|Wi-Fiのパスワードをお願いします。|Wi-Fi no pasuwaado wo onegaishimasu.|WI-FI NO PA-su-waa-do WO O-ne-gai-shee-ma-su.}}
{{ja-JP|部屋に問題があります。|Heya ni mondai ga arimasu.|HEYA NI MON-dai GA A-ri-ma-su.}}
explanation: This is standard accommodation language for front-desk conversations. It is polite and easy to reuse.
